songs.title: J-J-Jam
songs.lyrics:
[Intro — whispered]
j… j… j…
(soft, quick — not 'juh')

[Hook 1 — kids chant]
J! J! J-j-j!
What begins with J?
J-j-jam! J-j-jam!
It's jam! (it's jam!)

[Hook 2 — kids chant]
J! J! J-j-j!
What begins with J?
J-j-jug! J-j-jug!
It's a jug! (a jug!)

[Hook 3 — kids chant]
J! J! J-j-j!
What begins with J?
J-j-jet! J-j-jet!
It's a jet! (a jet!)

[Breakdown — whispered, bass drops out]
j… j… j…
j-j-jar… j-j-jeep… j-j-juice…

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not J!
(j… j… j…)

[Final Hook — big]
J! J! J-j-j!
J-j-jacket! J-j-jacket!
It's a jacket, jacket, jacket!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The J sound is a soft quick affricate /j/ as in 'jam' — a light 'dzh' snapped short, NEVER 'jay' (letter name) and NEVER 'juh' (dragged schwa). The '(j… j… j…)' clicks are crisp and brief on the hat. Answer line 'It's a ___!' reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: Jam in the Jug
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(sticky… sticky…)

[Hook]
Jam in the jug! Jam in the jug!
Where is the jam? (where? where?)
Jam on the cat!

[Verse 2]
Jam in the jug! Jam in the jug!
Where is the jam? (where? where?)
Jam on the dog!

[Verse 3]
Jam in the jug! Jam in the jug!
Where is the jam? (where? where?)
Jam on the rat!

[Verse 4]
Jam in the jug! Jam in the jug!
Where is the jam? (where? where?)
Jam on Sam!

[Outro]
Jam on the potato!
(POTATO!)
Jam in the jug…
(sticky… shhh…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: One frame: 'Jam on the ___!' — the target swaps per verse (cat → dog → rat → Sam → potato). The 'Jam in the jug' hook is the anchor line, constant. Kids choir answers the (where? where?) echoes. Sticky, slow, playful.

spreads.artNote: A round ceramic jug alone under the spotlight, lid off, waiting. Something is about to go wrong.
Thick red jam oozing up and over the rim of the jug, spilling down the sides. Glorious mess incoming.
The Cat (tabby) with a splat of red jam across its face, licking one paw, offended and delighted at once.
The Dog (brown) with jam all over its snout and ears, tail wagging, thrilled.
The Rat (grey) sitting IN a puddle of jam, whiskers dripping, tiny and outraged.
Sam the peg-doll boy with a handprint of jam on his red shirt, holding the sticky spoon, guilty grin.
A little toy jet plane nose-down and stuck in a pool of jam, wings sticking up. Absurd.
Sam mid-run, jam footprints trailing behind him across the floor, fleeing the sticky scene.
The potato, spotlit, sitting proudly in the middle of a big red pool of jam. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS the word from the picture.

sentences: The jet is in the jam.
extras: Moveable alphabet: build j-a-m, j-e-t, j-u-g, j-o-g — note the tail of j drops below the line
Real jam tasting: a spoon of jam on toast while sounding out 'jam' — the anchor word you can eat
Sticky sort: J objects (jam, jug, jet, jar) into the sticky basket, everything else stays out

REUSED FROM WEEK 1 — a potato wearing tiny round glasses standing at a miniature whiteboard with a pointer, teaching, deadpan, ultra-realistic photograph, single subject centered, dramatic spotlight on deep forest-green backdrop, soft shadows, cinematic, slightly whimsical --ar 3:2 --style raw

teacherFocus: Introduce /j/ as a soft quick sound (jam), never dragged into 'juh'. Blend j-a-m, j-e-t, j-u-g, j-o-g and notice the tail of j hanging below the line — the first descender letter after g. Taste real jam while sounding the anchor word. The sticky-mess book is pure joy; let them predict who gets jam next.
